goal: Build a mixed-SKU graduated tower: 3 cubes stacked vertically with decreasing size (10cm bottom, 8cm middle, 5cm top). Uses compute_stack_placement v3 cube_sizes for cumulative-z column stacking. Realizes the original 'CP-09 Graduated Tower' research description (sequence-constrained stacking, strict descending order).
thoughts:
Six graduated-tower specifics for CP-15 (extends CP-13 + CP-12 — mixed-SKU column):
1) compute_stack_placement v3 cube_sizes=[0.10, 0.08, 0.05] yields cumulative-z positions: bottom (10cm) at z=0.85, middle (8cm) at z=0.94, top (5cm) at z=1.005. Each cube center accounts for ALL lower cubes' full heights.
2) Pallet 0.20×0.20×0.05m at [0, -0.4, 0.775] — bigger than CP-13's 15×15cm because 10cm bottom cube needs 5cm+ margin.
3) Picking order: controller's _next_cube picks closest to sensor. Cube_1 (biggest, x=-1.4) is furthest left → belt carries it to sensor first. Cube_2 (8cm, x=-1.0) next. Cube_3 (5cm, x=-0.6) last. Physical-order matches stacking order (descending).
4) Top cube center at z=1.005 (top of tower at z=1.03). Robot base at z=0.75 → reach to top cube xy=(0, -0.4): sqrt(0.16+0.0676) = 0.475m. Within 0.85m reach.
5) EE_INITIAL_HEIGHT auto = max(source_z, max_drop_z) + 0.20 = 1.005 + 0.20 = 1.205m. EE clears tallest target during transit.
6) Cube_2 placement on top of Cube_1 (10cm cube top at z=0.90) — Cube_2 (8cm) bottom at z=0.94-0.04=0.90. Flush. Same for Cube_3 on Cube_2.
code:
# DomeLight + Ground + table
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.0, 0.5, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)

create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    position=[0.0, 0.4, 0.78],
    size=[3.0, 0.4, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.2, 0, 0],
)

# 3 cubes with decreasing size — biggest first (x=-1.4) so picked first via belt
cube_specs = [
    ("/World/Cube_1", -1.4, 0.10),  # 10cm — bottom
    ("/World/Cube_2", -1.0, 0.08),  # 8cm — middle
    ("/World/Cube_3", -0.6, 0.05),  # 5cm — top
]
for path, x, sz in cube_specs:
    z = 0.805 + sz * 0.5
    create_prim(prim_path=path, prim_type="Cube", position=[x, 0.4, z], size=sz)
    for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=path, schema_name=api)

bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=[c[0] for c in cube_specs],
    attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)
for c in cube_specs:
    apply_physics_material(prim_path=c[0], material_name="rubber")

# Pallet base 0.20x0.20x0.05m
create_prim(prim_path="/World/PalletBase", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, -0.4, 0.775], scale=[0.10, 0.10, 0.025])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/PalletBase", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

add_proximity_sensor(sensor_path="/World/PickSensor", position=[0.4, 0.4, 0.835], size=[0.06, 0.06, 0.06])

# Mixed-SKU column drop_targets via compute_stack_placement v3:
#   compute_stack_placement(target='/World/PalletBase', pattern='column',
#                            n_items=3, cube_sizes=[0.10, 0.08, 0.05], anchor='top')
# Yields:
#   Cube_1 (10cm): z=0.85   (0.80 + 0.05)
#   Cube_2 (8cm):  z=0.94   (0.80 + 0.10 + 0.04)
#   Cube_3 (5cm):  z=1.005  (0.80 + 0.10 + 0.08 + 0.025)
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path="/World/PickSensor",
    belt_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    source_paths=["/World/Cube_1", "/World/Cube_2", "/World/Cube_3"],
    destination_path="/World/PalletBase",
    drop_targets={
        "/World/Cube_1": [0.0, -0.4, 0.85],
        "/World/Cube_2": [0.0, -0.4, 0.94],
        "/World/Cube_3": [0.0, -0.4, 1.005],
    },
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/ConveyorBelt", "/World/PalletBase"],
)
extension_notes: CP-15 realizes the research-defined 'Graduated Tower' canonical (sequence-constrained stacking with strict descending order). First canonical truly using compute_stack_placement v3's cube_sizes feature for mixed-SKU column. Robot picks biggest-first (Cube_1 at conveyor-far-left enters reach first via belt motion).
failure_modes: Cube_1 (10cm) gripper-width mismatch — Franka's ParallelGripper opens to ~4cm; 10cm cube can be gripped on its 10cm face but the fingers contact at half-width. Risky but possible. Real industrial gripper would have wider opening.
Cube_2 / Cube_3 placed off-center → tower tips after 3rd cube. PalletBase 20cm gives 5cm margin around 10cm cube — adequate.
Picking order disrupted by belt timing — if Cube_1 (10cm, slow belt speed due to weight) lags behind Cube_2 (8cm), order may flip. Use sleepThreshold=0 + rubber material to keep timing predictable.
Top cube reach z=1.005 — 0.255m above robot base (z=0.75). Within Franka's vertical reach 0.85m. Fine.
